headline: launchd plist scheduling daily PSI calibration cache refresh (unattended-safe)
problem_statement: ω-network-6 shipped tool/psi_calibration_cache.hexa with 24h TTL self-refresh — but it only refreshes when an operator sources it. Cron-driven hexa_remote calls bypass the source path, so the cache could remain stale indefinitely under unattended ops.
fix_summary: Committed launchd plist that runs the existing tool with --refresh-force once per day at 04:20, ensuring state/psi_calibration_active.tsv stays inside the 24h TTL window without operator interaction.
design_decisions: Hour=4 Minute=20 chosen to differ from com.nexus.bridge_pipeline's :15 cadence — avoids simultaneous I/O.
Nice=5 (vs bridge_pipeline's Nice=1) — housekeeping should yield to monitoring pipeline.

Direct hexa-binary invocation (not /bin/bash wrapper) since target is a .hexa tool — no shell intermediary needed; mirrors the spirit of bridge_pipeline (which uses /bin/bash because its target is a .sh).
RunAtLoad=false per requirement — operator bootstrap is decoupled from execution; first scheduled fire is at next 04:20.
Did not pass --quiet (psi_calibration_cache.hexa has no --quiet flag); --refresh-force is the only flag the tool consumes for forced-refresh semantics.
Separate install-help tool (psi_calibration_install_help.hexa) keeps concerns isolated from bridge_pipeline_install_help.hexa per ω-bridge-20 precedent.

constraints_honored.hexa_only_suffix_raw9: OK — only new files are .plist (system-defined) and .hexa.
constraints_honored.no_parallel_session_collision: OK — did not touch cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own.
constraints_honored.state_read_only: OK — no writes to state/* (the tool itself writes state/psi_calibration_active.tsv, but that's pre-existing ω-network-6 behavior and not a 6b mutation).
constraints_honored.explicit_path_commit: OK — git add by file paths only.
constraints_honored.no_anima_commit: OK — closure JSON is untracked at /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/.
constraints_honored.no_launchctl_load: OK — only file commit; operator-initiated bootstrap.

follow_ups: If parallel-session activity around hexa_remote stabilizes, consider re-pointing hexa_remote to source state/psi_calibration_active.tsv directly, removing the 'eval source-on-every-shell' operator burden.
Optional: extend bridge_anomaly to surface __PSI_CALIBRATION__ FALLBACK sentinel from the daily log, escalating on 2+ consecutive fallback days.
